articles.description: An early warning system for sepsis, one of the deadliest infections for hospital patients, has been approved for use by the FDA, one of the first AI-based medical tools to get clearance. articles.title: Anker’s fast, foldable 3-in-1 charging station is back to its best price yet
articles.description:
Anker’s foldable charging station lets you charge three devices simultaneously. | Image: Anker	

Anker’s Prime Wireless Charging Station is a small, yet powerful 3-in-1 charger that can charge your iPhone, Apple Watch, and earbuds simultaneously, and now it’s on sale for $104.99 ($45 off) at Amazon, Best Buy, and Walmart. The price has only dropped this low one other time since it launched in February.
Anker Prime Wireless Charging Station (3-in-1, MagGo, AirCool, Foldable)

Where to Buy:

 $149.99 $104.99 at Amazon
 $149.99 $104.99 at Walmart
 $149.99 $104.99 at Best Buy

There are a few things that set this charger apart, other than its diminutive size. The foldable charger features a Qi2.2 wireless charging pad capable of delivering up to 25W of power to the iPhone 16 and newer models. According to Anker, it can charge an iPhone 17 Pro from 0 to 50 percent in under 30 minutes. Anker’s “AirCool” cooling system for the phone’s charging pad helps to prevent overheating, protect your battery, and maintain the fastest-possible charging speeds.
Beyond quickly charging compatible phones, the gadget is also capable of recharging Qi-compatible earbuds and an Apple Watch simultaneously. It can double as an adjustable phone stand so you can prop your phone up while charging to watch videos or check notifications. Anker also includes everything you need in the box, including a 45W USB-C wall charger and a five-foot USB-C-to-USB-C cable.
Three more ways to save
JBL’s Flip 7 speaker is on sale for $99.95 ($50 off) at Amazon, Best Buy, and Target, matching its best price to date. The Bluetooth speaker is rugged, thanks to its IP68 rating for water and dust resistance, and its battery can last up to 16 hours before it needs to be recharged. The speaker includes tech designed to help deliver clearer sound with less distortion at high volumes. It also comes with a loop and carabiner that make it a more versatile Bluetooth speaker than most other models.
Now through May 21st, you can get three months of Crunchyroll’s Fan plan for $1.99 ($8 off) or the Mega Fan plan for $2.99 ($11 off) a month as a part of the company’s Ani-May Sale. Both plans grant you access to Crunchyroll’s massive library of anime, which include shows like One Piece, Naruto, Dragon Ball, and Daemons of the Shadow Realm. The Mega Fan tier lets you stream on up to four devices at a time instead of just one. It also increases Crunchyroll Store discounts to 10 percent from five.
JLab’s Go Pop ANC wireless earbuds are on sale in pink for $14.81 ($15 off) and blue for $17.92 ($11 off) respectively when you clip their on-page coupons at Amazon. The budget-friendly earbuds support active noise cancellation and can last up to 24 hours on a single charge. They are rated IP55, which means they can stand up to sweat and day-to-day moisture, like getting caught in some rain. They also boast multipoint connectivity and they ship in a charging case that includes a built-in USB-C cable.

articles.title: Heat-related hospitalizations rose over two decades. Black adults, low-income communities disproportionately affected
articles.description: A study examined racial and ethnic disparities in rates of heat-related illness (HRI) hospitalizations among U.S. adults from 1998 to 2022. The findings showed that hospitalization rates for HRIs have increased overall in the U.S., with Black adults experiencing the highest rates and greatest increases. People residing in low-income ZIP codes were also disproportionately affected. The findings are published in the Annals of Internal Medicine.

articles.title: Google’s Aluminium OS revealed in 16-minute leaked video
articles.description:
Mystic Leaks’s video shows “Android” as the OS in the Settings app. | Image: Mystic Leaks via Android Authority	

Aluminium OS, Google's upcoming version of Android for PC, may have just leaked a few hours before Google's Android Show presentation. As Android Authority reports, leaker Mystic Leaks shared a 16-minute video on their Telegram channel that appears to show a lengthy hands-on demo of the new operating system. 
After a loading screen featuring the Android logo, the video shows the setup process for a new device. That process and the UI overall both look very similar to stock Android throughout, only resized to suit a laptop rather than a phone. The taskbar and app drawer are similar to what ChromeOS users will be familiar with, but the defaul …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: There’s an internet choke point in the Middle East — is the solution in the North Pole?
articles.description:
The vast majority of the world's data - emails, financial transactions, the internet - is carried by fiber optic cables that run along the ocean floor and converge at a few narrow choke points. Periodically, policymakers will release reports noting that this arrangement seems risky, but these routes are the shortest, often in use since the telegraph era, and the system has managed remarkably well. Cables break regularly, and traffic gets rerouted until a repair ship can come and fix the cut. But the war in Iran, coming after several years of disruptions from conflict in Yemen, is spurring governments and companies to consider alternate route …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Amazon’s ultrafast 30-minute deliveries are now available in more cities
articles.description:
Items like groceries, medicines, and electronics are now available in less than an hour. | Image: Amazon	

Following a limited test run in parts of Seattle and Philadelphia that started last December, Amazon Now is officially available to millions of Amazon shoppers across the United States. In addition to Seattle and Philadelphia, the 30-minute delivery service is now also widely available in Atlanta and Dallas-Fort Worth, but will soon be expanding to more cities including Austin, Houston, Minneapolis, Orlando, Phoenix, Denver, and Oklahoma City.
There are currently thousands of items eligible for 30-minute deliveries including fresh milk and produce, over-the-counter medicines, household essentials like laundry detergent, and even small elect …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Waymo recalls robotaxis for driving on flooded roads
articles.description:
A Waymo drives across Congress Avenue on 8th Street in front of the Capitol Building as rain arrives in the Austin area on Friday, Jan. 23, 2025 ahead of anticipated drops in temperature and freezing rain over the weekend. (Sara Diggins/The Austin American-Statesman via Getty Images) | Image: Getty	

Waymo is recalling its autonomous driving software that allowed its vehicles to drive on flooded roads. The recall affects 3,791 vehicles that operate using Waymo's fifth and sixth generation systems. 
In documents filed with the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, the Alphabet-owned company said that an unoccupied Waymo robotaxi "encountered an untraversable flooded section of a roadway that has a 40 mph speed limit." Despite detecting the flooded road, the vehicle "proceeded at reduced speed." Waymo said it was currently working on a remedy. In the interim, the company has updated its vehicles to "increase weather-related cons …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Canvas owner reaches ‘agreement’ with hackers to secure stolen data
articles.description:
Instructure, the company behind the Canvas learning management platform, says it has "reached an agreement" with hackers that breached its systems last week to prevent stolen data from being leaked online. 
The ShinyHunters hacking group claimed responsibility for the attack before Canvas was briefly taken offline. The group threatened to publish 3.5 terabytes of student data if ransom demands for a "settlement" weren't met. Now, Instructure says the stolen data has been returned as part of its unspecified "agreement" with the hackers, alongside a promise that "no Instructure customers will be extorted as a result of this incident."
"We un …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Vodafone Idea board to weigh fundraise through equity after AGR relief
articles.description: Vodafone Idea board will consider raising funds via equity shares and warrants as sentiment improves after AGR relief and regulatory clarity. The stock has rallied sharply in recent months on reduced dues, leadership changes and stake-related developments. Brokerages turn more positive, citing improved visibility for fundraising and network expansion plans.

articles.title: Hegseth to face questions on Iran, Europe in defense budget hearings
articles.description: The defense secretary appears before Congress as a ceasefire with Tehran falters and lawmakers object to the withdrawal of 5,000 troops from Germany.

articles.title: The Download: a Nobel winner on AI, and the case for fixing everything
articles.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Three things in AI to watch, according to a Nobel-winning economist A few months before he won the Nobel Prize in economics in 2024, Daron Acemoglu published a paper that earned…

articles.title: WATCH:  K9 to the rescue
articles.description: How a police dog became a hero in the search for a missing elderly man and how it was all caught on camera.

articles.title: Carbon credits are flawed, but they can still help save forests
articles.description: Carbon credits bought by companies to offset their emissions really have reduced deforestation, but not by as much as credit developers claim, according to a rigorous analysis

articles.title: NEET-UG 2026 cancelled LIVE: Nashik police take paper leak accused into custody
articles.description: The cancellation of the NEET-UG 2026 examination, following an alleged question paper leak, has led to widespread political and student outrage across States

articles.title: AI super rally has retail investors acting the most aggressive since trading frenzy during Covid
articles.description: Retail traders are buying calls in Cboe's "Mag 10" stocks – the big seven plus AMD, Palantir and Broadcom – at the heaviest 10-day clip since 2021, according to a report from the exchange.

articles.title: Nahid Rana rips through Pakistan to seal a final-session win for Bangladesh
articles.description: This is now Bangladesh's third consecutive win against Pakistan, after they won the 2024 series by 2-0 margin

articles.title: Justice Department defends subpoenas of Wall Street Journal reporters
articles.description: The Justice Department defended itself after the Wall Street Journal revealed​ it has received subpoenas in connection with a leak investigation.

articles.title: Florida judge rules prosecutors can access Tiger Woods' prescription drug history after DUI arrest: report
articles.description: Tiger Woods' prescription drug records will reportedly be released to prosecutors after a Florida judge ruled on the subpoena tied to his DUI case.

articles.title: Godavari water to be released by late May for Kharif operations
FDA approves early warning system for sepsis
articles.description: An early warning system for sepsis, one of the deadliest infections for hospital patients, has been approved for use by the FDA, one of the first AI-based medical tools to get clearance. The tool, developed by Johns Hopkins University researchers and now commercialized by Bayesian Health, detects sepsis hours faster than doctors and has reduced deaths by nearly 20%.

articles.title: Research into Friedreich's ataxia reveals how DNA folding can silence a key gene
articles.description: Researchers have uncovered a fundamental rule that governs how genes are physically arranged inside the cell nucleus, and how disruptions to that organization can contribute to human disease.

articles.title: Healthcare staff at the forefront in Telangana’s Heatwave Action Plan
'Astonishing' skill development - Watson sees a young Samson in Raghuvanshi
articles.description: Raghuvanshi's numbers in the IPL are a bit sobering but Watson believes he will continue to develop into a well-rounded batter

articles.title: Alphabet Offers Eight-Tranche Deal in Its Debut Yen Bond Sale
articles.description: Alphabet Inc. has begun marketing its first-ever bond sale in yen, further broadening its funding channels as the Google parent sharply increases capital spending to finance artificial intelligence efforts.

topStories.title: The summer box office is off to a hot start as weekend ticket sales top $160 million
topStories.description: The summer box office is off to a sizzling start, thanks in part to strong week-after-week performance from returning titles.

topStories.title: Trust vote ahead, signs of cracks in Vijay's TN alliance over astrologer as OSD. But he's got ‘rebels’ as lifeline
topStories.description: Astrologer Rickey Radhan Pandit Vettrivel's appointment as Officer on Special Duty (Political) to the CM sparked reactions from allies Congress, VCK and Left.

topStories.title: 'Country expects us to get on with governing': Starmer refuses to quit as PM amid growing demands for his resignation
topStories.description: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer has issued a defiant message as calls grow seeking his resignation. The development comes as 80 Labour lawmakers have called for him to step down, with several resigning from their positions on Tuesday.

topStories.title: Gov Abbott extends off-ramp for NY billionaires fleeing Mamdani's policies
topStories.description: Gov. Greg Abbott promotes Texas as a pro-growth alternative as NYC Mayor Mamdani's policies targeting wealthy executives spark fears of business exodus.

topStories.title: Flyers star Owen Tippett reveals he missed series vs Hurricanes due to internal bleeding
topStories.description: Philadelphia Flyers star Owen Tippett revealed he missed the team's second-round series against the Carolina Hurricanes due to an internal bleeding issue.

topStories.title: US Soy Growers Want Commitments From China as Season Slips Away
topStories.description: As US President Donald Trump travels to Beijing for a summit this week with his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping, American soybean farmers are in a now-familiar spot: Fields are getting planted when demand from their biggest customer is uncertain.

topStories.title: New York could ban flour used to make bagels and pizza
topStories.description: New York legislators are weighing a ban on potassium bromate, an additive in flour used to make bagels, pizza, and other goods, due to its potential to cause cancer. Dr. Mark Hyman joins CBS News with more details.

topStories.title: EXCLUSIVE: SPLC's 'far-left' 'anti-racism' curriculum found in classrooms as early as kindergarten: watchdog
topStories.description: Education watchdog says SPLC integrates far-left content into classrooms in over 40 states as the group faces federal fraud charges in court.

topStories.title: Heat-related hospitalizations rose over two decades. Black adults, low-income communities disproportionately affected
topStories.description: A study examined racial and ethnic disparities in rates of heat-related illness (HRI) hospitalizations among U.S. adults from 1998 to 2022. The findings showed that hospitalization rates for HRIs have increased overall in the U.S., with Black adults experiencing the highest rates and greatest increases. People residing in low-income ZIP codes were also disproportionately affected. The findings are published in the Annals of Internal Medicine.

topStories.title: US household debt ticks up to new all-time high as inflation continues to rise
topStories.description: U.S. household debt, including mortgages, credit cards, auto loans and student loans, reached an all-time high of $18.8 trillion in the first three months of the year.

dailyNews.title: Lawmakers grill Hegseth over huge Pentagon budget request
dailyNews.description: Pentagon officials are on Capitol Hill to make the case for a $1.5 trillion 2027 budget amid the Iran war. CBS News' Nikole Killion reports.

dailyNews.title: Eric Clapton hit in the chest by fan-thrown object, cuts concert short
dailyNews.description: Eric Clapton was hit in the chest by what appeared to be a vinyl record during his Madrid concert, leaving early without performing his usual encore.

dailyNews.title: Search for Nancy Guthrie reaches 100 days as DNA evidence is probed
dailyNews.description: The search for Nancy Guthrie has spanned 100 days, and key evidence in the case is still under analysis. CBS News' Anna Schecter reports.

dailyNews.title: Trump Hints At 'More Complex Tariffs' After Latest US Court Defeat
dailyNews.description: Split US trade court ruling declares Donald Trump's global tariffs illegal, prompting the former President to hint at a revamped and potentially harsher tariff strategy.

dailyNews.title: Mother’s Day dinner dispute turns violent as man accused of stabbing grandmother 11 times: police
dailyNews.description: A 29-year-old Florida man allegedly stabbed his grandmother 11 times during an argument over Mother's Day dinner, police say. She remains critical.

dailyNews.title: 'Wife's career choice not cruelty or desertion': SC slams 'feudal' mindset of lower courts
dailyNews.description: The Supreme Court has set aside lower courts' regressive findings, stating a qualified woman's pursuit of her profession and child's welfare cannot be deemed cruelty or desertion. The apex court emphasised that marriage doesn't erase a woman's individuality or subordinate her identity, upholding divorce solely on irretrievable breakdown.

dailyNews.title: Ship operators involved in Baltimore bridge collapse charged with misconduct
dailyNews.description: The ship operators in connection to the collapse of Baltimore's Francis Scott Key Bridge, Synergy Marine Pte Ltd and Synergy Maritime Pte Ltd and one of its managers, have been charged with misconduct and obstruction. NBC News' Tom Costello reports on details of the indictment in connection with the collision that killed six people.

dailyNews.title: It's not just Iran and oil raising inflation. Prices also are reaccelerating in these other areas
dailyNews.description: Here's some of the places where prices are rising fast for consumers.

dailyNews.description: Amazon Now is coming to "dozens" of locations in 2026.

dailyNews.title: STAT+: STAT examines America’s deadliest drug
dailyNews.description: STAT examines America's failure to address excessive alcohol use across decades.

dailyNews.description: The leaders' upcoming meeting in Beijing has revived the idea of a 'Group of Two' between the superpowers.

dailyNews.description: The federal government indicted two foreign companies Tuesday in connection with the shipping boat crash that struck down the Francis Scott Key Bridge in 2024.

dailyNews.description: The Republican, whom President Donald Trump has endorsed for another term, has denied domestic violence and other allegations and sought to portray them as politically motivated.
